decisions.title: Prioritize Data Governance (DAG) architecture completion over new feature work
decisions.context: Multiple DAG-related tickets (ENG-10105, ENG-10106, DOC-13, DOC-19, ENG-9733) indicate ongoing governance infrastructure work. Need to decide if this blocks Embed Deeper initiatives or runs parallel.

arguments.for: DAG is foundational for enterprise deployment (Embed Deeper pillar). Customers need granular permissions before deep platform integration. Core infrastructure should be stable before building atop it.
arguments.against: Customer-facing features (Canvas, Explore V2) drive immediate decisions captured. DAG work is invisible to end users. Risk delaying North Star metric improvement.

decisions.title: Commit to file format expansion (TDMS, CH10, HDF5) for Q2 delivery
decisions.context: Three parallel client-side parsing implementations (ENG-9980, ENG-9969, ENG-9968) plus adapter pattern (ENG-10107). Major Nail the Core investment in data ingestion flexibility.

arguments.for: Aerospace/defense customers demand these formats (signal from customer calls). Removes ingestion friction, increases decisions captured per session. Clear moat-building differentiator.
arguments.against: High engineering cost for niche formats. Resources diverted from Python Rules v2 or Sift Edge improvements. May not move North Star metric if adoption is narrow.

decisions.title: Resolve kafka-archiver staging instability before production rollout
decisions.context: ENG-10101 indicates infrastructure instability. Kafka-archiver likely supports event streaming for Ask SIFT or real-time alerting (Build the Moat). Staging issues risk production incidents.
decisions.blockedBy: Root cause not yet identified in staging environment

arguments.for: Production reliability is non-negotiable. Instability in event streaming undermines real-time decision capabilities. Blocks Sift Edge deployment if dependent.
arguments.against: May be staging-specific configuration issue. Delaying for perfect stability risks missing Q2 feature gates. Can deploy with rollback plan.

decisions.title: Sunset enhanced channel search feature flag and commit to new search UX
decisions.context: Two cleanup tickets (ENG-10104, ENG-10058) removing feature flag references. Suggests channel search v2 is ready for full rollout. Impacts Explore V2 user experience.

arguments.for: Feature flags create technical debt and A/B test confusion. New search likely improves time-to-insight (Nail the Core). Clean rollout enables better onboarding.
arguments.against: If rollback needed, requires code deployment vs. config change. May have edge cases not caught in beta. Risk disrupting power users' workflows.

decisions.title: Accelerate Canvas Y-axis log scale and axes dropzones to April release
decisions.context: ENG-10108 (log scale fast follows) and ENG-10007 (axes dropzones) are UI enhancements for Canvas. Small features with high UX impact for data exploration workflows.

arguments.for: Log scale is table-stakes for engineering data visualization. Dropzones reduce click friction, increasing decisions captured per session. Quick wins for Nail the Core.
arguments.against: Incremental improvements don't unlock new use cases. Engineering effort better spent on stat panels (ENG-5489) or family stats (ENG-7853) with broader impact.

keyResults.name: Explore V2 adoption rate (% of sessions)

timeAudit.weekSummary: 62% meeting load—above sustainable threshold. Three 1:1s today suggest reactive firefighting vs. strategic planning. Only 2 focus blocks for deep work on file format strategy or DAG architecture review.
alerts: Zero calendar time allocated for Q2 roadmap finalization (due April 15)
Back-to-back meetings 1pm-5pm today block decision documentation
No engineering sync scheduled this week despite 8 backend infrastructure tickets
